players.name: Utkir Yusupov
players.bio: Veteran shot-stopper and Navbahor stalwart, Yusupov brings 40 caps of hard-earned experience to Uzbekistan's first-ever World Cup. At 35, he's proof that good things come to those who wait — even entire tournaments.
players.facts: At 35, Yusupov is one of Uzbekistan's oldest players at their debut World Cup — worth the wait!

players.name: Abdukodir Khusanov
players.bio: Born on 29 February 2004, Khusanov is literally a once-every-four-years phenomenon — fitting for a World Cup debutant. The Manchester City centre-back is one of European football's most exciting young defenders.
players.facts: Born on 29 Feb 2004, Khusanov only has a 'real' birthday every four years — a World Cup kid in every sense.

players.name: Khojiakbar Alijonov
players.bio: A dependable Pakhtakor cornerstone, Alijonov brings 40 caps and a rare goalscoring touch for a defender. Solid, experienced, and quietly crucial, he's the kind of player every backline needs but few headlines mention.
players.facts: With 2 international goals, Alijonov scores more than most defenders dare — a set-piece threat in disguise.

players.name: Farrukh Sayfiev
players.bio: One of Uzbekistan's most capped defenders, Sayfiev has been a reliable presence for over a decade. At 35, this Neftchi Fergana stalwart gets to cap a long career on football's grandest stage.
players.facts: Sayfiev and Yusupov share almost the same birthday — both born in January 1991, just 13 days apart.

players.name: Rustam Ashurmatov
players.bio: A composed centre-back plying his trade at Iranian giants Esteghlal, Ashurmatov is a cornerstone of Uzbekistan's defence with 49 caps. Calm under pressure and strong in the air, he's the White Wolves' defensive backbone.
players.facts: Ashurmatov plays his club football in Iran, making him well-drilled against some of Asia's toughest strikers.

players.name: Akmal Mozgovoy
players.bio: A combative and energetic midfielder for Pakhtakor, Mozgovoy brings industry and intelligence to Uzbekistan's engine room. His name translates roughly to 'brainy' in Russian — and his reading of the game suggests it fits.
players.facts: 'Mozgovoy' means 'brainy' in Russian — whether coincidence or destiny, his midfield IQ backs it up.

players.name: Otabek Shukurov
players.bio: Uzbekistan's most experienced outfield player at this tournament, Shukurov's 84 caps tell the story of a decade of tireless service. The UAE-based midfielder is creative, experienced, and still delivering on the big stage.
players.facts: With 84 caps, Shukurov is Uzbekistan's appearance machine — he's played more games than some nations have won.

players.name: Jamshid Iskanderov
players.bio: A cultured central midfielder with 38 caps and four international goals, Iskanderov is the heartbeat of Uzbekistan's midfield. Technically gifted and a veteran of countless Asian campaigns, he arrives at the World Cup battle-hardened.
players.facts: Iskanderov has been a key Uzbekistan midfielder for nearly a decade — a true servant of the White Wolves.

players.name: Odiljon Hamrobekov
players.bio: A tireless workhorse with 72 caps, Hamrobekov is one of Uzbekistan's most trusted midfielders. Playing in Iran with Tractor, he's built for the physical demands of international football and never stops running.
players.facts: Hamrobekov has 72 caps but just 1 goal — suggesting his job is winning the ball, not finishing with it.

players.name: Jaloliddin Masharipov
players.bio: The creative heartbeat of the White Wolves, Masharipov's silky dribbling and 12 international goals make him Uzbekistan's most dangerous attacking midfielder. The Esteghlal star is the player opponents fear most.
players.facts: Masharipov wears the iconic No.10 shirt and has 12 international goals — Uzbekistan's creative talisman.

players.name: Oston Urunov
players.bio: One of Uzbekistan's most exciting young talents, Urunov has already bagged 10 international goals at just 25. Playing for Persepolis in Iran, he's got pace, flair, and a knack for the spectacular.
players.facts: Urunov scored 10 goals before turning 25 — not bad for someone who was a teenager when he debuted.

players.name: Abduvohid Nematov
players.bio: Nasaf's young goalkeeper Nematov is only 25 with 8 caps, but has earned his seat on Uzbekistan's World Cup plane. A calm and composed shot-stopper, he's learning his craft behind some experienced competition.
players.facts: Nematov is one of the younger keepers at this World Cup — still building his cap tally at a historic tournament.

players.name: Sherzod Nasrullaev
players.bio: A reliable and versatile defender for Nasaf, Nasrullaev has 31 caps and a couple of goals to his name — rare currency for a backline player. Solid in the tackle and composed on the ball, he's a trusted squad option.
players.facts: Nasrullaev has scored 2 goals from defence — more than several Uzbekistan midfielders can claim.

players.name: Eldor Shomurodov
players.bio: Uzbekistan's all-time leading scorer and talismanic striker, Shomurodov has 44 goals in 92 caps. A powerful, mobile forward who has graced Serie A with Roma, he is the focal point of every White Wolves attack.
players.facts: Shomurodov is Uzbekistan's all-time top scorer with 44 goals — he's basically a one-man highlight reel.

players.name: Umar Eshmurodov
players.bio: A seasoned defender with 29 caps, Eshmurodov is one of the quieter but essential figures in Uzbekistan's backline. At 33, the Nasaf man brings veteran composure to a squad experiencing World Cup football for the first time.
players.facts: At 33, Eshmurodov is one of the elder statesmen of the squad — calming first-timers with every touch.

players.name: Botirali Ergashev
players.bio: Third-choice keeper Ergashev has just 2 caps but is no stranger to competitive football with Neftchi Fergana. At 30, he's a steady, experienced professional who brings solid backup to the goalkeeping department.
players.facts: Ergashev has just 2 caps but made the World Cup squad — proving patience pays off in the long run.

players.name: Dostonbek Khamdamov
players.bio: A technically capable and energetic Pakhtakor midfielder, Khamdamov chips in with goals as well as graft — 5 in 34 caps is a solid return. A clever, box-to-box operator who thrives in Uzbekistan's midfield structure.
players.facts: Khamdamov has 5 international goals from midfield — not bad for someone whose first job is defensive coverage.

players.name: Abdulla Abdullaev
players.bio: Abdullaev is a steady defensive presence with 17 caps, currently playing in the UAE with Dibba. A dependable squad option who provides cover and professionalism across the backline at Uzbekistan's debut World Cup.
players.facts: Abdullaev plays in the UAE — his desert-heat training could prove useful in a North American summer.

players.name: Azizjon Ganiev
players.bio: A creative midfielder with 19 caps, Ganiev plays his club football in the UAE with Al Bataeh. Still developing his international impact, he provides fresh legs and technical ability off the bench for the White Wolves.
players.facts: Ganiev is yet to score internationally but brings creative energy — his moment may come in North America.

players.name: Azizbek Amonov
players.bio: A local hero at Dinamo Samarqand, Amonov has shown enough quality to earn 12 caps and 2 international goals. An energetic forward with sharp instincts in front of goal, he's a wildcard option Uzbekistan can rely on.
players.facts: Amonov plays for Dinamo Samarqand — representing a city once at the heart of the ancient Silk Road.

players.name: Igor Sergeev
players.bio: One of Uzbekistan's most prolific attackers, Sergeev has scored 25 goals in 83 caps — a remarkable record for a Russian-named Uzbek international. The Persepolis forward is a smart, clinical finisher with real World Cup pedigree.
players.facts: Sergeev is one of very few players named Igor to represent Uzbekistan — and one of their deadliest ever scorers.

players.name: Abbosbek Fayzullaev
players.full_name: Abbosbek Sayidjon oʻgʻli Fayzullayev; Файзуллаев Аббосбек Саиджон угли
players.bio: Born in Sirdaryo in 2003, Fayzullaev is Uzbekistan's electrifying young star. The İstanbul Başakşehir attacking midfielder has 8 goals in 32 caps and the kind of dribbling ability that makes defenders nervous.
players.facts: Fayzullaev was born in 2003 and already has 8 international goals — a generational talent still warming up.

players.name: Sherzod Esanov
players.full_name: Sherzod Dilshodovich Esanov; Эсанов Шерзод Дилшод угли
players.bio: A central midfielder from Navoi with just one senior cap, Esanov is Uzbekistan's wildcard inclusion. The Bukhara youngster represents the future of White Wolves football — his World Cup debut could be one for the ages.
players.facts: Esanov has just 1 senior cap heading into the World Cup — the ultimate baptism of fire for any young player.

players.name: Bekhruz Karimov
players.bio: At just 18, Karimov is one of the youngest players at the entire 2026 World Cup. The Tashkent-born right-back from Surkhon Termiz has barely started his international career — but what a place to begin it.
players.facts: Born in 2007, Karimov could be one of the youngest players at World Cup 2026 — a teen on the world stage.

players.name: Avazbek Ulmasaliev
players.full_name: Avazbek Alisher oʻgʻli Oʻlmasaliev; Авазбек Алишер оглы Ўлмасалиев
players.bio: Uncapped heading into the tournament, Ulmasaliev earns his first World Cup call-up from AGMK. The Fergana-born centre-back is a bold selection — but Uzbekistan's historic debut is the perfect stage for a debut too.
players.facts: Ulmasaliev arrives at the World Cup with 0 caps — making him potentially one of the tournament's biggest debutants.

players.name: Jakhongir Urozov
players.bio: A young centre-back from Viloyat Jizzax, Urozov has 4 caps at just 22 and is one of Uzbekistan's brightest defensive prospects. The Dinamo Samarqand defender brings youthful energy and growing confidence to the squad.
players.facts: Urozov is just 22 and born in Jizzax — a city known for its harsh winters. He won't fear tough conditions.
